tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every single test is text/pattern matching on source files (regex searches on .zig files and .test.ts files). No test compiles the code, runs bun install, or executes any behavioral check. All tests are variations of 'assert pattern in open(path).read()'.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for specific code patterns that don't exist in the base commit (e.g., the empty 'else => {}' must be replaced, Output calls must be added to multiple branches, Output calls must be removed from security_scanner.zig). A stub/no-op wouldn't pass these.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction requirement 1 says 'Every security scanner error path must print a descriptive error message to stderr before exiting' but no test actually runs bun install and checks stderr output. The tests only verify source code patterns.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive docstrings, grouped into sections (fail-to-pass pr_diff, fail-to-pass agent_config, pass-to-pass) with clear comments.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, function names, and variable names are consistent between instruction.md and test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction explicitly names 'Output.errGeneric' as the required function (requirement 2), names 'SecurityScannerRetryFailed' as the error to avoid (requirement 3), and specifies that 'only SecurityScannerInWorkspace errors produce output' — these are implementation-level details that guide the agent to the exact solution pattern.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agentmd-edits task.
